title: PixelPerfect: Responsive Architecture Challenge
description: Build a professional, fully responsive landing page for a futuristic AI agency. The focus is not on JS functionality, but on absolute layout precision and responsive behavior across all screen sizes using modern CSS (Grid, Flex, Variables).
requirements: A sticky navigation bar with a logo (left) and links (right) that collapses into a burger menu on mobile.
A 'Hero Section' with a two-column layout: Text/CTA on the left and a decorative element on the right. Must stack vertically on mobile.
A 'Features Grid' containing 6 cards. Must transition from 3 columns (Desktop) $ightarrow$ 2 columns (Tablet) $ightarrow$ 1 column (Mobile) using CSS Grid.
A complex pricing table with 3 tiers, where the 'Pro' tier is visually highlighted (scaled up slightly).
Strict use of CSS Variables for the color palette (Primary, Secondary, Accent, Background).
Zero overflow-x issues on any screen width (tested from 320px to 2560px).

milestones.title: Design Tokens & Reset
tasks: Define all brand colors in :root variables

milestones.title: Core Layout Skeleton
tasks: Build the Navbar and Hero section using Flexbox
Create the Features Grid using grid-template-columns
Ensure the layout is 'fluid' using percentages or fr units

milestones.title: Responsive Refinement
tasks: Write media queries for Tablet (768px) and Mobile (480px)
Implement the mobile burger menu (CSS-only toggle)
Optimize image sizes using responsive attributes

stretchGoals: Implement 'Dark Mode' using a data-theme attribute and CSS variables.
Add smooth scroll behavior to all internal links.
Create a complex CSS animation for the hero element (e.g., floating effect).

evaluationRubric.responsiveness: Does the page layout shift logically? Are there any horizontal scrollbars on mobile?
evaluationRubric.technicalPrecision: Are CSS Grid and Flexbox used correctly? Is there an over-reliance on fixed pixel widths?
evaluationRubric.codeHygiene: Is the CSS organized logically? Are variables used instead of hardcoded hex codes?
